title: 1.1: Tests that recipient display is derived from alias email only when alias has no display name and no custom domain display name
1.2: Tests that recipient display includes alias name in standard formatted form with guidance for from header when alias has a display name
1.3: Tests that alias name takes precedence for recipient display when alias has a display name and custom domain also has a display name
1.4: Tests that recipient display is alias email only when alias has no display name and custom domain has no display name
1.5: Tests that recipient display uses custom domain default name in standard formatted form with from header guidance when alias has no display name but custom domain has a display name
2.1: Places the test for recipient display when alias has no overrides in tests/test_alias_utils.py
2.2: Places the test for recipient display when alias has a display name in tests/test_alias_utils.py
2.3: Places the test for recipient display when custom domain supplies the name in tests/test_alias_utils.py
3.1: All added tests use the pytest framework
3.2: All added tests follow the test_<name> naming pattern
3.3: All added tests use assert statements to verify behavior
4.1: Follows the pattern of creating user and alias (or custom domain) test data before asserting on recipient display for the recipient-display tests
